designRule: Gender identity influence is optional presentation style only. It is never biological determinism or stereotype.

genderIdentities.name: Cis-Male
genderIdentities.voiceDesignSummary: A grounded register option that can emphasize lower center, steadier cadence, and firmer sentence endings when desired.
genderIdentities.nonEssentialistUseNote: This is an optional fictional voice-performance influence. It must not be treated as a rule for how real people with this identity speak.
genderIdentities.accentInteraction: Gender identity modifies register placement, pitch movement, emotional color, and layering while preserving the biome accent and race/class articulation rules.
performanceRules: Keep accent features audible, but let authority and low resonance slightly thicken the carrier.
Use fewer upward question-like endings unless personality calls for them.

genderIdentities.name: Cis-Female
genderIdentities.voiceDesignSummary: A bright, expressive register option that can emphasize warmth, clearer pitch motion, and smoother emotional color.
genderIdentities.nonEssentialistUseNote: This is an optional fictional voice-performance influence. It must not be treated as a rule for how real people with this identity speak.
genderIdentities.accentInteraction: Gender identity modifies register placement, pitch movement, emotional color, and layering while preserving the biome accent and race/class articulation rules.
performanceRules: Allow accent vowels to open clearly and keep emotional inflection audible.
Avoid over-thinning; maintain race/body resonance where present.

genderIdentities.name: Demi-Male
genderIdentities.voiceDesignSummary: A partially masc-coded performance option with grounded warmth but less strongly lowered than a full masc register.
genderIdentities.nonEssentialistUseNote: This is an optional fictional voice-performance influence. It must not be treated as a rule for how real people with this identity speak.
genderIdentities.accentInteraction: Gender identity modifies register placement, pitch movement, emotional color, and layering while preserving the biome accent and race/class articulation rules.
performanceRules: Blend steadiness with flexible warmth.
Keep the biome accent clear rather than overly heavy.

genderIdentities.name: Demi-Female
genderIdentities.voiceDesignSummary: A partially femme-coded performance option with refined lightness, gentle warmth, and precise but softened contour.
genderIdentities.nonEssentialistUseNote: This is an optional fictional voice-performance influence. It must not be treated as a rule for how real people with this identity speak.
genderIdentities.accentInteraction: Gender identity modifies register placement, pitch movement, emotional color, and layering while preserving the biome accent and race/class articulation rules.
performanceRules: Soften hard consonants slightly unless race/class intentionally restores them.
Use controlled rises and careful phrase shaping.

genderIdentities.name: Non-Binary
genderIdentities.voiceDesignSummary: A centered register option that balances bright and low cues without committing the performance to a strongly gendered pitch profile.
genderIdentities.nonEssentialistUseNote: This is an optional fictional voice-performance influence. It must not be treated as a rule for how real people with this identity speak.
genderIdentities.accentInteraction: Gender identity modifies register placement, pitch movement, emotional color, and layering while preserving the biome accent and race/class articulation rules.
performanceRules: Keep pitch center flexible and let personality/race/class do more of the identifying work.
Use balanced resonance and avoid default gendered exaggeration.

genderIdentities.name: Trans-Male
genderIdentities.voiceDesignSummary: A masc-aligned performance option that can emphasize affirming grounded resonance, controlled depth, and strength without flattening emotion.
genderIdentities.nonEssentialistUseNote: This is an optional fictional voice-performance influence. It must not be treated as a rule for how real people with this identity speak.
genderIdentities.accentInteraction: Gender identity modifies register placement, pitch movement, emotional color, and layering while preserving the biome accent and race/class articulation rules.
performanceRules: Maintain emotional availability under the lower center.
Let chosen identity support confidence while still preserving accent clarity.

genderIdentities.name: Trans-Female
genderIdentities.voiceDesignSummary: A femme-aligned performance option that can emphasize lift, emotional brightness, and careful resonance while preserving strength and depth where needed.
genderIdentities.nonEssentialistUseNote: This is an optional fictional voice-performance influence. It must not be treated as a rule for how real people with this identity speak.
genderIdentities.accentInteraction: Gender identity modifies register placement, pitch movement, emotional color, and layering while preserving the biome accent and race/class articulation rules.
performanceRules: Lift intonation and warmth without erasing race/class weight.
Use breath and legato as expression, not weakness.

genderIdentities.name: Gender-Fluid
genderIdentities.voiceDesignSummary: A dynamic performance option with moving register, shifting formants, and expressive transitions across scenes or emotional states.
genderIdentities.nonEssentialistUseNote: This is an optional fictional voice-performance influence. It must not be treated as a rule for how real people with this identity speak.
genderIdentities.accentInteraction: Gender identity modifies register placement, pitch movement, emotional color, and layering while preserving the biome accent and race/class articulation rules.
performanceRules: Allow register and vowel color to move over time.
Use scene or emotion to select current voice placement rather than one fixed center.

genderIdentities.name: Agender
genderIdentities.voiceDesignSummary: A less gender-coded performance option with centered register, reduced gendered pitch cues, and clean controlled articulation.
genderIdentities.nonEssentialistUseNote: This is an optional fictional voice-performance influence. It must not be treated as a rule for how real people with this identity speak.
genderIdentities.accentInteraction: Gender identity modifies register placement, pitch movement, emotional color, and layering while preserving the biome accent and race/class articulation rules.
performanceRules: Keep identity cues intentionally neutral and let mood/personality carry expression.
Avoid exaggerated masc/femme pitch signatures.

genderIdentities.name: Gender-Less
genderIdentities.voiceDesignSummary: A deliberately ungendered performance option that can sound steadier, more abstract, mechanical, ancient, alien, or spiritually detached depending on race/class.
genderIdentities.nonEssentialistUseNote: This is an optional fictional voice-performance influence. It must not be treated as a rule for how real people with this identity speak.
genderIdentities.accentInteraction: Gender identity modifies register placement, pitch movement, emotional color, and layering while preserving the biome accent and race/class articulation rules.
performanceRules: Use steadier intonation and less gendered resonance movement.
For constructs, undead, spirits, or planar beings, this can become intentionally uncanny but still intelligible.

genderIdentities.name: Gender-Flexible
genderIdentities.voiceDesignSummary: A performance option that can lean softer or firmer as context changes while remaining recognizably the same person.
genderIdentities.nonEssentialistUseNote: This is an optional fictional voice-performance influence. It must not be treated as a rule for how real people with this identity speak.
genderIdentities.accentInteraction: Gender identity modifies register placement, pitch movement, emotional color, and layering while preserving the biome accent and race/class articulation rules.
performanceRules: Let delivery move between firm and tender registers.
Keep biome accent stable so the character remains recognizable.

genderIdentities.name: Bi-Gender
genderIdentities.voiceDesignSummary: A layered performance option with two identifiable register colors that can alternate, blend, or harmonize in emphasis.
genderIdentities.nonEssentialistUseNote: This is an optional fictional voice-performance influence. It must not be treated as a rule for how real people with this identity speak.
genderIdentities.accentInteraction: Gender identity modifies register placement, pitch movement, emotional color, and layering while preserving the biome accent and race/class articulation rules.
performanceRules: Use two-register layering: one brighter/warmer, one deeper/steadier.
Switch or blend depending on mood, line importance, or magical emphasis.

genderIdentities.name: Poly-Gender
genderIdentities.voiceDesignSummary: A many-layered performance option that can stack multiple register colors, especially useful for magical, draconic, fey, or cosmic characters.
genderIdentities.nonEssentialistUseNote: This is an optional fictional voice-performance influence. It must not be treated as a rule for how real people with this identity speak.
genderIdentities.accentInteraction: Gender identity modifies register placement, pitch movement, emotional color, and layering while preserving the biome accent and race/class articulation rules.
performanceRules: Use multi-register harmonics or alternating phrase colors.
Keep the main spoken line intelligible while additional layers color the identity.

genderIdentities.name: Neutrois
genderIdentities.voiceDesignSummary: A neutral-gender performance option that balances warmth, clarity, and composure with minimal binary pitch coding.
genderIdentities.nonEssentialistUseNote: This is an optional fictional voice-performance influence. It must not be treated as a rule for how real people with this identity speak.
genderIdentities.accentInteraction: Gender identity modifies register placement, pitch movement, emotional color, and layering while preserving the biome accent and race/class articulation rules.
performanceRules: Favor balanced resonance, measured inflection, and clean emotional sincerity.
Avoid making neutrality emotionless unless personality requires it.
